title: Luxury Penthouse Lounge Configurator
description: Users design a luxury penthouse lounge by positioning a glamorous velvet sofa within an architectural space featuring dramatic god ray lighting streaming through floor-to-ceiling windows. The configurator allows adjusting sofa placement, rotation, upholstery color, god ray intensity, time-of-day lighting, and camera viewpoints to compose the perfect interior scene.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements a luxury penthouse lounge configurator. Load GlamVelvetSofa.glb as the centerpiece furniture and godrays_demo.glb as the architectural environment providing volumetric light shafts. Setup: Place the godrays_demo model as the room environment at the origin. Position the sofa centered on a reflective floor plane (MeshStandardMaterial with metalness 0.9, roughness 0.1). Add a PerspectiveCamera at (0, 2.5, 6) looking at the sofa. Include ambient light (intensity 0.3) and a directional light simulating sunlight that casts shadows. Enable shadow maps on the renderer. Interactions (7 steps with validation): 1) DRAG TO POSITION: Click and drag the sofa along the floor plane (raycaster constrained to Y=0). Clamp X to [-4,4] and Z to [-4,4]. Display coordinates in a HUD overlay. 2) ROTATE SOFA: Press R key or use a slider (0-360 degrees) to rotate the sofa around Y-axis. Show current angle. 3) CHANGE UPHOLSTERY: Provide 5 color swatches (deep crimson #8B0000, royal purple #4B0082, emerald #006400, midnight blue #191970, champagne gold #C5B358). Clicking a swatch traverses the sofa's meshes and sets their material color. Validate the color actually changed. 4) GOD RAY INTENSITY: A slider (0.0-2.0, default 1.0) scales the opacity/emissive intensity of all materials in the godrays_demo model. 5) TIME OF DAY: A slider (6-20 representing hours) adjusts the directional light color temperature (warm orange at 6/20, white at 12) and angle. 6) CAMERA PRESETS: Three buttons — Front View (0,2,6), Top Down (0,8,0.1), Dramatic Low (3,0.5,4) — animate the camera using smooth lerp over 60 frames. 7) SCREENSHOT: A capture button uses renderer.domElement.toDataURL() to trigger a download. Build a styled HTML panel on the left side with all controls. Use OrbitControls for free camera movement between presets. All parameter changes must reflect immediately in the 3D scene.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  sofaPositionX: number,         // X coordinate of sofa center on floor plane, clamped [-4, 4]
  sofaPositionZ: number,         // Z coordinate of sofa center on floor plane, clamped [-4, 4]
  sofaRotationY: number,         // Sofa rotation around Y-axis in degrees [0-360]
  upholsteryColorHex: string,         // Current upholstery color as hex string (#8B0000, #4B0082, #006400, #191970, #C5B358)
  godRayIntensity: number,         // Volumetric light intensity multiplier [0.0-2.0]
  timeOfDay: number,         // Time simulation value affecting directional light angle [0-24 hours]
  shadowsEnabled: boolean,         // Whether shadow rendering is active
  floorReflectivity: number,         // Floor material metalness value [0.0-1.0]
  cameraDistance: number,         // Distance of camera from sofa center in meters
  interactionMode: string,         // Current interaction state (idle, dragging, rotating, colorPicking)
  totalColorChanges: number,         // Count of upholstery color changes made
  validPlacementZone: boolean,         // Whether sofa is within valid floor bounds
}
